clause_text: The contract price is fixed for the entire duration of the project and shall not be subject to any adjustment for any reason whatsoever.
risk_reason: A fixed-price clause in a multi-year construction contract with no price adjustment mechanism exposes the contractor to inflation and material cost volatility.
saudi_legal_note: التضخم وتذبذب أسعار مواد البناء قد يُخلّ بالتوازن المالي للعقد — يُنصح بربط السعر بمؤشر أسعار المستهلك أو تكاليف البناء في العقود التي تتجاوز 18 شهرًا.
recommended_revision: Add a price adjustment clause tied to the Saudi CPI or a specified materials index; applicable annually after the first 12 months of the contract.
related_regulation: Saudi Civil Transactions Law (Royal Decree M/191 1444H)

clause_text: In the event of delay by the Contractor, liquidated damages equal to the full remaining contract value shall be payable for each calendar month of delay.
risk_reason: Liquidated damages equal to the remaining contract value per month are grossly disproportionate and constitute a punitive penalty rather than a genuine pre-estimate of loss.
saudi_legal_note: المادة 111 من نظام المعاملات المدنية تُجيز للقضاء تخفيض الغرامة التعسفية — لكن هذا البند يُكبّل المقاول ماليًا ويُعرّضه لخسارة تتجاوز قيمة العقد.
recommended_revision: Revise to: liquidated damages capped at 10% of total contract value in aggregate; accruing at 0.5% of remaining value per week of delay after the agreed cure period.
related_regulation: Saudi Civil Transactions Law (Royal Decree M/191 1444H)

clause_text: Any dispute arising from this Agreement shall be finally resolved by arbitration in London under the ICC Rules.
risk_reason: London ICC arbitration is impractical and costly for a Saudi construction project; enforcement of the award in the Kingdom requires compliance with Saudi Arbitration Law.
saudi_legal_note: نظام التحكيم السعودي (المرسوم الملكي م/34 1433هـ) يحكم تنفيذ أحكام التحكيم الأجنبي في المملكة — التحكيم في لندن يُضاعف التكاليف ويُعقّد تنفيذ الحكم محليًا.
recommended_revision: Revise to: arbitration seated in Riyadh under the Saudi Center for Commercial Arbitration (SCCA) Rules; Saudi law as governing law.

clause_text: The contract contains no provisions addressing force majeure events or relief for the Contractor in cases of unforeseeable disruption.
risk_reason: Absence of a force majeure clause leaves the contractor exposed to liability for delays caused by events beyond their control such as extreme weather or government orders.
saudi_legal_note: نظام المعاملات المدنية السعودي يعترف بمبدأ القوة القاهرة — لكن غياب تعريفها التعاقدي يُفتح باب الخلاف حول ما يُعدّ قوة قاهرة في قطاع الإنشاءات.
recommended_revision: Add a force majeure clause defining qualifying events; notification timelines (5 business days); and remedies (time extension and cost relief; or termination after 90 days of sustained disruption).
related_regulation: Saudi Civil Transactions Law (Royal Decree M/191 1444H)
